response.justification: The candidate is a strong hands-on senior engineer with roughly 19 years of broad experience across web, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, meeting the tenure requirement. However, the role is a director-level IC focused on AI strategy, and the resume shows only recent, tooling-level AI exposure (MCP generators, agent infrastructure since 2025), no recognized AI expertise or AI-built products, no advanced AI-related degree, and no evidence of operating at principal/director scope such as setting technical strategy, advising executives or strategic customers, or leading cross-organizational initiatives.
strengths: 19+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency distributed systems
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of an open-source geo-data platform used in production by NGOs
Active knowledge-sharing through conference talks, workshops, and courses, aligned with the inspire/evangelize responsibility
concerns: Minimal AI/ML depth — recent agent-tooling work only, with no demonstrated AI products, research, or recognized expertise
No principal- or director-level scope; career has stayed at senior/staff IC delivery rather than organizational or strategic leadership
Limited cloud platform strategy experience — cloud usage is operational (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) rather than shaping future cloud product direction

key_factors.factor: AI expertise gap vs. AI-strategy core requirement
Long, versatile engineering tenure with entrepreneurial open-source work
